diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Pseudoachalasia due to adenocarcinoma of the cardia
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient presented with symptoms and findings initially thought to be idiopathic achalasia, including dysphagia, weight loss, and manometric findings of aperistalsis and incomplete LES relaxation. However, further investigations including endosonography and biopsy revealed a tumor involving all layers of the esophageal wall, leading to the diagnosis of pseudoachalasia secondary to adenocarcinoma of the cardia. The failure of botulinum toxin injection to improve symptoms also supported the diagnosis of pseudoachalasia over idiopathic achalasia.
